text: The door swings open. A girl with worm-segment earrings walks in holding a coffee cup. She scans the room — you, Vermia, Grubwin — and arrives at a look of calm, practiced sympathy directed entirely at you.

speaker: Mawla
text: New one?

speaker: Vermia
text: New DEVOTEE.

speaker: Mawla
text: Mawla. I joined this club eight months ago because it seemed funny. I'm still here. I've stopped asking questions about that.

speaker: Mawla
text: The poems are actually pretty good though. That part's real.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Mawla is our best writer. She won't say that herself, but it's true.

speaker: Mawla
text: I write okay poems about a worm deity. The bar is specific.

speaker: Vermia
text: They are TRANSCENDENT poems. The one you wrote about Scatha's burrowing? I cried.

speaker: Mawla
text: You cry at everything, Vermia.

speaker: Vermia
text: I am spiritually open!
Mawla sits down at a desk near the window and opens a notebook. She doesn't say anything else. Somehow this is the most comfortable energy in the room.

speaker: Vermia
text: OKAY! Now that everyone is here — oh wait, where's Dirtus?

speaker: Grubwin
text: He texted me. He says he'll be late because he's 'preparing something important for the club'.

speaker: Mawla
text: That's terrifying.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Yeah.

speaker: Vermia
text: It's EXCITING. Dirtus always brings passion to the club! Even if last time the passion was a three-meter scroll of hymn lyrics that he'd glued together with actual glue that was still wet.

speaker: Grubwin
text: It got on my clipboard.

speaker: Vermia
text: Scatha works in mysterious ways.
Vermia claps her hands together and turns to face you with the full force of her enthusiasm.

speaker: Vermia
text: Alright! [player]! Today is a very special day because you are joining us, which means it is YOUR first poem day! Every new member writes a poem for Scatha on their first day!

speaker: [player]
text: I've never written a poem before.

speaker: Vermia
text: Scatha doesn't care about skill. He cares about SINCERITY.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Also you can just write about something you like. It doesn't have to be about Scatha specifically. The theme is broad.

speaker: Vermia
text: The theme is Scatha. It's always Scatha.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Last week you gave me positive feedback on a poem I wrote about my grandmother.

speaker: Vermia
text: Your grandmother had wormy energy. It was an honorary Scatha poem.

speaker: Mawla
text: Just write whatever feels true. That's all any poem is.
She says it simply, not looking up from her notebook. But somehow it lands.
Vermia hands you a piece of paper and a pen. It smells faintly of soil.
